narrative.quote: Despite strong Friday shipping at $109,928 and Monday's projected $339K burn, we face 4 consecutive days where incoming orders exceed outbound shipments, pushing backlog to $1.33M. Critical supplier delays with Bethyl creating direct production risk.

operationalRealities.title: Incoming Orders Outpacing Shipments
operationalRealities.detail: Four consecutive days of negative burn rate with Friday showing +$107K net backlog increase

operationalRealities.title: Warehouse Execution Strong
operationalRealities.detail: Friday achieved 100% OTD with 427 Monday shipments confirmed, no additional pack support needed

operationalRealities.title: Critical Supplier Delays
operationalRealities.detail: Bethyl $22K order delayed to 3/26 due to QC issues, creating direct production risk

strategyUpdates: New receiving/stocking process piloted with receiver inputting location directly into AX while stocker focuses on physical placement only, though lot number visibility gap identified requiring immediate solution.

Aggressive Monday ship plan targeting $339K through combination of $164K in stock, $83K incoming, $42K COD manufacturing, and $50K projected same-day orders - potentially the strongest single-day burn of the month.

actionItems.task: Monitor and report on new stocking process effectiveness
Drive solution for lot number visibility gap in new receiving process
Update backlog tracking daily and confirm MTD OTD in morning email
Contact customer Renna on 71-line cancellation request and report back with recommendation
Confirm Bethyl $6K PO availability and continue supplier ETA follow-up
Provide clearance delay status update on all 27 pieces
